session.assistant:
Wait — I realize there's a problem with my approach for `view/cli.py`. The human CLI re-fetches the issue just for display metadata (title, state, timestamps), but the operation already fetches it internally. Let me rethink this — I should have the operation return the issue metadata as part of the result, or better yet, keep the issue metadata in the result so the human CLI doesn't need to re-fetch. But the plan says "Result dataclasses should carry rich domain objects."
Let me revise the operation to include issue metadata in the result, so the human CLI doesn't need to re-fetch:
session.tool_result:

1→"""Save plan as objective GitHub issue. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec objective-save-to-issue [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→This command extracts a plan and creates a GitHub issue with: 7→- erk-objective label only (NOT erk-pr - objectives are not plans) 8→- No title suffix 9→- Plan content directly in body (no metadata block) 10→- No commands section 11→ 12→Options: 13→ --session-id ID: Session ID for scoped plan lookup 14→ --format: json (default) or display 15→ --validate: Run objective validation after creation 16→ 17→Exit Codes: 18→ 0: Success - objective issue created 19→ 1: Error - no plan found, gh failure, etc. 20→""" 21→ 22→import json 23→from pathlib import Path 24→from typing import Any, cast 25→ 26→import click 27→ 28→from erk.cli.commands.objective.check_cmd import ( 29→ ObjectiveValidationError, 30→ ObjectiveValidationSuccess,
... [10 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Fetch, validate, and set up context for objective-plan command. 2→ 3→Consolidates data fetching, validation, marker creation, and roadmap 4→serialization into a single exec command, eliminating the Task agent. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk exec objective-plan-setup 3679 --session-id SESSION_ID 8→ 9→Output: 10→ JSON with objective data, roadmap, validation results, and marker status. 11→ 12→Exit Codes: 13→ 0: Success 14→ 1: Error (not found, wrong label, validation error) 15→""" 16→ 17→import json 18→from pathlib import Path 19→ 20→import click 21→ 22→from erk.cli.commands.objective.check_cmd import ( 23→ ERK_OBJECTIVE_LABEL, 24→ ObjectiveValidationError, 25→ validate_objective, 26→) 27→from erk.cli.repo_resolution import get_remote_github 28→from erk_shared.context.helpers import require_context, require_repo_root 29→from erk_shared.context.types import NoRepoSentinel 30→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueNotFound
